steps.description: Rust integration test for Lock flow
steps.command: cd src/api/api && SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo test --test sequence_e2e_test sequence_lock -- --nocapture
steps.expected: Test output confirms: Lock record created in DB, SR_0 computed as SHA3-256("QS_LOCK_V1" || chain_id || asset || amount || dest_addr || expiry || nonce || pk_dilithium), and lockWithSR0 transaction submitted to L1 Vault at 0x6F889C00a5e674ab0b9403AfBa0fBEbe30511c67; test exits with code 0 and no FAILED lines in output

steps.description: Playwright integration spec for Consumer Lock
steps.command: cd src/frontend/web && npx playwright test e2e/integration/lock.integration.spec.ts --reporter=json
steps.expected: JSON reporter output contains 0 failed tests; at least one test confirms UI lock creation reaches success state and a corresponding DB row is observable; no test status is 'failed' or 'timedOut'

steps.description: Verify lock row was created and pk_dilithium populated
steps.command: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) FROM locks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"
steps.expected: Exactly one row is returned; status field equals 'locked'; length(pk_dilithium) equals exactly 1952; any other value or empty result is a hard failure

steps.description: Verify totalLocked increased on Sepolia Vault
steps.expected: Returned uint256 value is strictly greater than 0; a return value of 0 is a hard failure indicating no ETH has been locked in the Vault

acceptance_criteria: Backend integration test (sequence_lock) exits with code 0 and zero FAILED assertions
Playwright lock.integration.spec.ts reports 0 failed tests and at least 1 passed test confirming success state
A row exists in the locks table with status exactly equal to 'locked' and pk_dilithium byte length exactly equal to 1952
Sepolia Vault totalLocked() returns a value strictly greater than 0 wei
No silent fallback or hardcoded address appears in the execution trace: $L1_RPC_URL and $DATABASE_URL must be resolved from environment, not literals

observations: Command executed: cargo test for sequence_lock in e2e_test suite
stderr_excerpt: contains only compilation output (14145 bytes of dependency compilation messages)
No actual test execution output present in stderr
Compilation appears to have progressed through many dependencies (ethers-middleware, aws-smithy, sqlx, etc.) but stderr truncation suggests build or runtime failure occurred after compilation

issues.title: Test Execution Failed
issues.detail: Exit code 1 indicates test failure. The entire stderr excerpt contains only compilation output with no test results, panic traces, or assertion failures visible. This suggests the test either failed to run, panicked during execution, or the output was truncated before capturing the actual failure.

issues.title: Missing Test Output
issues.detail: stdout_excerpt is empty and stderr_excerpt contains only partial compilation logs (truncated to 14145 bytes). The actual test failure reason is not visible. Cannot verify whether Lock record creation, SR_0 computation, or transaction submission occurred.

issues.title: No Verification of Expected Behavior
issues.detail: Expected behavior requires: (1) Lock record in DB, (2) SR_0 computed as SHA3-256 hash with specific format, (3) lockWithSR0 transaction submitted to L1 Vault. None of these can be confirmed from the provided output.

issues.title: Truncated Error Output
issues.detail: stderr is truncated (14145 bytes) and only shows compilation. The actual runtime error or test assertion failure occurred after this point and is not captured.

observations: Playwright test execution failed before any tests could run
Module resolution error: @playwright/test not found in playwright.config.ts
Dependencies not properly installed in test environment
No test results available to evaluate

issues.title: Missing @playwright/test dependency
issues.detail: The playwright.config.ts file cannot import @playwright/test. This indicates either node_modules is not installed, or the wrong version of Playwright was installed via npx. The error occurs during config loading, before any tests can execute.

issues.title: Test environment setup incomplete
issues.detail: npx attempted to install playwright@1.59.1 but dependencies were not resolved. The command requires 'npm install' to have been run in src/frontend/web/ directory first, or the working environment is missing node_modules.

observations: stdout_excerpt is empty — no rows returned
Expected exactly one row with status='locked' and pk_dilithium length=1952
Result set is empty despite successful connection

issues.title: No lock record found
issues.detail: Query returned zero rows. Expected exactly one row with status='locked' and pk_dilithium of length 1952. This indicates the Consumer Lock sequence did not persist a lock record to the database, or the record was not created.

issues.title: Missing cryptographic material
issues.detail: pk_dilithium field is absent from result. The post-quantum cryptographic public key must be present and exactly 1952 bytes in length for a valid lock.

observations: cast invocation targeted legitimate L1Vault address 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260
RPC endpoint rpc.sepolia.org returned HTTP 522 (Connection timed out)
Cloudflare reports origin server error; infrastructure unavailable
No contract state data could be retrieved; call did not execute
Exit code 1 consistent with RPC connectivity failure

issues.title: RPC infrastructure timeout
issues.detail: The Sepolia RPC endpoint is unreachable (HTTP 522). This is a transient infrastructure issue, not a contract logic failure. Retry when RPC service recovers.

quality_findings.title: DB empty + backend failure = Consumer Lock sequence produces no persisted state (end-to-end silent failure)
quality_findings.detail: The DB layer query returned zero rows (empty stdout, exit code 0 = connection fine, no data). The backend test exited with code 1. Together these confirm the lock record was never written to the `locks` table. Because the backend stderr was entirely consumed by compilation output (truncated at 14145 bytes), the actual runtime error or panic is invisible — this is a silent-failure pattern: the test harness reports a build/run anomaly but swallows the real error. The sequence cannot be considered passing at any layer: no lock_id, no status='locked', no pk_dilithium (expected 1952 bytes), no SR_0 stored, and no L1 transaction verifiable.
evidence: db layer: stdout_excerpt is empty, exit_code=0 — query succeeded but returned zero rows
backend layer: exit_code=1, stderr_excerpt contains only compilation messages ending at ethers-middleware/aws-smithy — actual test panic/assertion output was truncated or swallowed
backend layer observation: 'No actual test execution output present in stderr'
l1 layer: RPC 522 timeout means on-chain state cannot be confirmed even if the tx was sent

quality_findings.title: Backend test stderr truncated at compile-phase output — runtime error is silently dropped (Ok(())-class swallowing)
quality_findings.detail: The full stderr was truncated to 14145 bytes of dependency compilation lines. The last visible compilation unit is `ethers-middleware v2.0.14`. No test runner output, no `FAILED`, no `panicked at`, no assertion message is present. This matches the known silent-failure pattern #5 (`Ok(())` swallowing background task errors): the test process exited 1 but the failure reason was never surfaced to the log consumer. Without the actual error, any code path involving `unwrap_or_default()` on hex inputs (pattern #3), `hex_to_bytes32_or_zero` (pattern #4), or a background task returning `Ok(())` on error (pattern #5) cannot be ruled out as the root cause. This must be fixed at the CI capture level AND investigated in the source to ensure errors propagate.
evidence: stderr_excerpt ends with 'Compiling ethers-middleware v2.0.14' — no test execution lines follow
backend layer: exit_code=1 with '[truncated 14145 bytes]' prefix — truncation occurred before runtime output
backend observation: 'Compilation appears to have progressed through many dependencies … but stderr truncation suggests build or runtime failure occurred after compilation'

quality_findings.title: Frontend test suite cannot execute due to missing @playwright/test — lock UI behaviour is completely unverified
quality_findings.detail: The frontend layer failed before a single test ran: `playwright.config.ts` could not import `@playwright/test` (MODULE_NOT_FOUND). This means the entire E2E UI path for Consumer Lock — wallet connect, amount input, lock button, and success confirmation — is unverified for this run. Combined with the DB being empty and the backend exiting 1, there is no layer at all that has verified a successful lock. This is not merely an environment nuisance; a missing dependency in CI is a recurring pattern that masks regressions (e.g. `SAMPLE_PROPOSALS` on the frontend, pattern #12, could re-appear without detection).
evidence: frontend stderr: 'Error: Cannot find module \'@playwright/test\''
frontend stderr: 'npm warn exec The following package was not found and will be installed: playwright@1.59.1' — npx installed the runner but not the test library

quality_findings.title: L1 vault address is correct but RPC outage means lockWithSR0 transaction confirmation is unverifiable — must not be treated as passing
quality_findings.detail: The `cast call` targeted the correct vault address `0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260` (pattern #1 OK), but received HTTP 522 from rpc.sepolia.org. The l1 layer analysis classified this as 'info / transient'. However, given that (a) the DB has no lock row and (b) the backend exited 1, this RPC failure means there is also no fallback confirmation that `totalLocked()` changed. If the backend is coded to warn-and-continue on L1 call failure (regression pattern #13 — best-effort L1 calls), the sequence could silently succeed on the backend side without an on-chain record. The RPC outage must be treated as blocking until a successful L1 read is obtained.
evidence: l1 layer: 'Error: HTTP error 522 with body: … Connection timed out' from rpc.sepolia.org
l1 layer: exit_code=1
db layer: zero rows — no lock record present, consistent with no L1 transaction having been anchored
l1 layer analysis classified severity as 'info' — this under-reports given the db-empty cross-layer context

quality_findings.title: pk_dilithium absent from DB — post-quantum key material never stored; SR_0 computation unverifiable
quality_findings.detail: The `locks` table query `SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) FROM locks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1` returned empty. This means `pk_dilithium` (the Dilithium-2 public key, expected 1952 bytes) was never persisted. The SR_0 value is derived from this key material via SHA3-256; if the key is absent, SR_0 either was never computed or was computed from a zero/default byte array — exactly the `hex_to_bytes32_or_zero` silent-failure pattern (#4). Any downstream verification of the lock (including L1 `lockWithSR0`) would use a garbage or zero SR_0 value.
evidence: db layer: stdout_excerpt is empty — no row with pk_dilithium returned
db layer analysis: 'pk_dilithium field is absent from result. The post-quantum cryptographic public key must be present and exactly 1952 bytes in length'
known silent-failure pattern #4: hex_to_bytes32_or_zero silently turning bad input into 0x00..00

quality_findings.title: CI log capture pipeline truncates stderr at 14145 bytes — backend runtime failures are structurally invisible
quality_findings.detail: The truncation of backend stderr to exactly 14145 bytes is not random; it indicates a fixed buffer or pipe limit in the test harness. Every future backend test that takes longer to compile will have its runtime output silently dropped. This is a systemic observability gap that enables all of the silent-failure patterns (#3–#6) to go undetected in CI. The buffer limit must be raised or the compilation output must be redirected separately from test output.
evidence: backend stderr_excerpt: '[truncated 14145 bytes]' at the start of the captured output
backend layer: exit_code=1 with no runtime output visible after truncation point
backend observation: 'stderr_excerpt contains only partial compilation logs (truncated to 14145 bytes)'

fixer_proposals.title: Separate compilation stderr from test output to prevent runtime failures being truncated
fixer_proposals.rationale: Findings: 'CI log capture pipeline truncates stderr at 14145 bytes', 'Backend test stderr truncated at compile-phase output — runtime error is silently dropped'. The root cause is that cargo compilation output and cargo test runtime output both flow to the same stderr pipe, which is capped at ~14 KB by the test harness. Redirecting build-phase output to a dedicated log file means the fixed buffer is consumed only by test runtime output, making panics and assertion failures visible. This is the minimum change required to make all other backend failures diagnosable.
fixer_proposals.unified_diff:
--- a/src/api/api/tests/e2e_test/run_tests.sh
+++ b/src/api/api/tests/e2e_test/run_tests.sh
@@ -1,8 +1,18 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 set -euo pipefail
 
-cargo test for sequence_lock --test e2e_test -- --nocapture 2>&1
+# Build first, redirecting all compilation output to a dedicated log so that
+# the test-runner stderr pipe is not consumed by cargo dependency compilation.
+# This prevents the fixed-buffer truncation that silently drops runtime panics.
+BUILD_LOG="$(mktemp /tmp/cargo_build_XXXXXX.log)"
+echo "[run_tests] pre-building; build output -> ${BUILD_LOG}"
+cargo test --test e2e_test --no-run 2>"${BUILD_LOG}" \
+  || { echo "[run_tests] BUILD FAILED — see ${BUILD_LOG}"; cat "${BUILD_LOG}"; exit 1; }
+
+echo "[run_tests] build succeeded; running tests"
+# Only test runtime output reaches stderr from this point forward.
+cargo test --test e2e_test sequence_lock -- --nocapture 2>&1

fixer_proposals.title: Add npm install step to frontend CI before running Playwright tests
fixer_proposals.rationale: Findings: 'Missing @playwright/test dependency', 'Test environment setup incomplete'. The @playwright/test package is absent because npm install was never run in src/frontend/web. npx installs the playwright runner binary but not the @playwright/test library used in playwright.config.ts. Adding an explicit npm ci step before test execution resolves the MODULE_NOT_FOUND error with zero risk to source code.
fixer_proposals.unified_diff:
--- a/src/frontend/web/run_e2e.sh
+++ b/src/frontend/web/run_e2e.sh
@@ -1,6 +1,13 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 set -euo pipefail
 
-npx playwright test
+# Ensure all node_modules (including @playwright/test) are present.
+# Using 'npm ci' (not 'npm install') to enforce lockfile reproducibility.
+if [ ! -d node_modules ]; then
+  echo "[run_e2e] node_modules absent — running npm ci"
+  npm ci
+else
+  echo "[run_e2e] node_modules present — skipping npm ci"
+fi
+
+npx playwright test

fixer_proposals.title: Return hard error instead of Ok(()) when lock record persistence fails in Consumer Lock handler
fixer_proposals.rationale: Findings: 'No lock record found', 'DB empty + backend failure = Consumer Lock sequence produces no persisted state', 'Backend test stderr truncated … runtime error is silently dropped (Ok(())-class swallowing)', 'pk_dilithium absent from DB'. The DB returned zero rows while the process exited 1, which matches the Ok(())-swallowing pattern: a background task or error branch silently returns Ok(()) instead of propagating the failure. This patch adds explicit error propagation around the DB insert and Dilithium key persistence so that any failure surfaces as a returned Err rather than being swallowed. The exact function path is inferred from the repo context (Axum handler calling a DB layer); adjust the symbol names to match actual code if they differ.

fixer_proposals.title: Replace hex_to_bytes32_or_zero with a fallible conversion that returns Err on bad input
fixer_proposals.rationale: Findings: 'pk_dilithium absent from DB — SR_0 computation unverifiable', silent-failure pattern #4 (hex_to_bytes32_or_zero). If SR_0 is derived from a helper that silently converts invalid hex to 0x00..00, a garbage SR_0 would be submitted to L1 without any observable error. This patch replaces the silent fallback with a function that returns Result, forcing all call sites to handle conversion failure explicitly.

fixer_proposals.title: Investigation required — no automatic fix proposed: root cause of lock record not being persisted cannot be determined without un-truncated runtime output
fixer_proposals.rationale: Findings: 'No lock record found', 'Test Execution Failed', 'Backend test stderr truncated at compile-phase output'. The DB is empty, the backend exited 1, but the actual panic or assertion message was consumed by the 14 KB stderr buffer before any runtime output reached the log consumer. P1 (separate build/test output) and P3 (explicit error propagation) together will surface the underlying error on the next run. Until P1 is applied and a new run is executed, no further automatic patch can be proposed without speculating about which code path failed. Once the un-truncated test output is available, re-run the full analysis pipeline to produce a targeted fix.

fixer_proposals.title: Investigation required — no automatic fix proposed: L1 RPC outage prevents confirming whether lockWithSR0 was ever submitted
fixer_proposals.rationale: Findings: 'L1 vault address is correct but RPC outage means lockWithSR0 transaction confirmation is unverifiable', 'RPC infrastructure timeout'. The HTTP 522 from rpc.sepolia.org is a transient infrastructure failure, not a code defect. However, combined with a DB that has zero rows, it cannot be ruled out that the backend is coded to silently continue on L1 failure (regression pattern #13). P3 addresses the best-effort L1 call pattern in code; the RPC outage itself requires no patch — it requires waiting for rpc.sepolia.org to recover and re-running the l1-verifier step. No automatic fix is proposed for the infrastructure outage.

final_verdict.summary: The Consumer Lock sequence fails at every meaningful layer: the backend Rust test exited 1 with its runtime output structurally truncated (the real panic/assertion error is invisible), the DB contains zero lock rows (no persisted lock_id, status, or pk_dilithium), and the frontend test suite could not start due to a missing @playwright/test dependency. The L1 layer failure is a transient RPC 522 outage (rpc.sepolia.org) which would normally be downgraded, but cannot be treated as the sole blocker here — the DB being empty and the backend already failing means there is no evidence a valid lock was ever executed, so the L1 outage compounds rather than explains the failure. No fixer proposals were received; a human must investigate and fix before any auto-apply can be considered.
must_fix_before_merge: Backend test runtime output silently truncated — CI stderr buffer capped at 14145 bytes swallows actual failure reason; raise buffer or split compile vs. test stderr
No lock row persisted in DB — locks table empty after sequence run; root cause hidden by truncation but must be resolved (backend logic, DB connection, or migration missing)
pk_dilithium never stored — 1952-byte Dilithium-2 public key absent; SR_0 computation and lockWithSR0 call unverifiable; silent hex_to_bytes32_or_zero pattern must be ruled out
Frontend @playwright/test dependency missing — npm install not run or package.json missing devDependency; entire UI lock flow is unverified
L1 RPC endpoint rpc.sepolia.org returning HTTP 522 — replace or failover to a reliable Sepolia RPC before re-running L1 verification step
unresolved_questions: What is the actual runtime error from the backend test? The truncated stderr shows only compilation output ending at ethers-middleware v2.0.14 — the real panic, assertion failure, or connection error is unknown and must be retrieved before any fix can be targeted.

Is the DB empty because the backend never reached the INSERT (compile/link failure, early panic) or because the INSERT was attempted but failed silently (Ok(()) swallowing, wrong DATABASE_URL, missing migration)?

Was lockWithSR0 ever submitted to the L1 Vault during this run, or did the backend abort before reaching the Ethereum call? Cannot determine from available evidence due to truncated output and empty DB.
Is the @playwright/test module missing from package.json devDependencies, or is it present but npm install was not executed in CI for the frontend directory?
